methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $2.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.75B

private.name: Ayar Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

data.narrative:
Lambda is a Series E AI & Software company founded by Stephen and Michael Balaban, currently valued at $2.5B post-money following $3.15B in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private frontier-tech companies at similar stages, with direct valuation benchmarks drawn from three same-sector, same-stage peers: Crusoe Energy ($10.0B), SandboxAQ ($5.8B), and Ayar Labs ($3.8B).

Two quantitative methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier to Lambda's $2.5B last post-money valuation, implying $3.5B. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the three-company cohort, which has a median valuation of $5.8B and a range of $3.8B to $10.0B. These approaches reflect typical venture expansion mechanics and direct market comparables, respectively.

Triangulation of the two methods produces a valuation range of $3.0B (low) to $6.6B (high), with a median of $4.6B. This range is materially wider than Lambda's current post-money, reflecting heterogeneity in peer outcomes at this stage. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peer cohorts, path to profitability, and the substantial dispersion within the peer set—a 2.6x spread from Ayar Labs to Crusoe Energy suggests stage-E valuations remain sensitive to differentiation in capital efficiency and market positioning.
